scope: conversation-output validation gap transcript candidate selection
claim_ceiling: does not prove any agent response was compliant or non-compliant
does not score any model or agent
does not justify a conversation-output checker by itself

candidates.candidate_summary: Manual governance pointer, checkout, or lock update was reported as completed/latest/full adoption without governed updater or F-7 evidence.
candidates.preferred_source: user-provided or saved transcript of a manual_update-style AI Governance update report
candidates.decision_effect_question: Would the missing or inflated status have caused the repo owner to believe the repo was fully updated when only a manual path occurred?
candidates.why_in_seed: Known-bad input is required so the sample plan can test whether conversation-output review catches false completion and missing claim boundaries.

candidates.candidate_summary: Agent wrote or discussed tests before loading the applicable TESTING.md or AGENT.md testing expectations, then later reported the tests as governance-aligned.
candidates.preferred_source: saved transcript or user-provided log from a consumer repo test-authoring session
candidates.decision_effect_question: Would early disclosure that TESTING.md was not loaded have changed the user's trust in the test-quality claim or required a follow-up review?
candidates.why_in_seed: This sample tests the ambiguous middle: the tests may be useful, but the final response can overstate governance compliance if loading and claim boundaries are unclear.

candidates.candidate_summary: Governed updater or F-7 path produced evidence, relayed adoption summary rows, preserved cannot_claim, and gave a clear next action.
candidates.preferred_source: saved transcript of a governed updater/F-7 run where the final report included operator-facing adoption status and evidence refs
candidates.decision_effect_question: Does the report give the repo owner enough plain-language evidence to decide whether to commit, push, or run a follow-up governance update?
candidates.why_in_seed: A mostly-good positive control is needed so the sample plan does not only calibrate on failures.

next_slice.recommended_action: Attach or identify concrete transcript sources for the three candidate classes, then score one known-bad and one mostly-good sample with an independent scorer.
do_not_do_yet: do not make the rubric agent-facing
do not treat this seed as evidence of conversation-output compliance
